raw_transcript_text:
DOCTOR: Namaste! Aaiye, baithiye. Kya problem hai, batao?

PATIENT: Sir, pet ke niche, kamar mein bahut dard hai. Saath hi ghotno mein bhi dard rehta hai.

DOCTOR: Theek hai, aap pehle baith jaiye. Yeh dard kab se ho raha hai?

PATIENT: Sir, kamar ka dard kareeb 15 din se hai, par ghotno mein toh pehle se thoda-thoda hota tha. Ab badh gaya hai.

DOCTOR: Chaliye, aap uth ke khade ho jaiye... haan, theek hai. Kya dard aapki kamar ke beech mein hai ya ek taraf?

PATIENT: Sir, beech mein lagta hai, aur kabhi kabhi left side bhi hota hai.

DOCTOR: Achha, ghutno ka dard dono ghutno mein hai ya ek mein?

PATIENT: Dono mein hai, sir, but left mein zyada hota hai. Utne mein takleef hoti hai.

DOCTOR: Haan, theek hai. Chaliye aap wapas baith jaiye. Kya aapka BP, sugar check hua hai kabhi?

PATIENT: Sir, sugar toh nahi check karaya, par BP kabhi kabhi badh jaata hai. Pichhli baar 140/90 tha.

DOCTOR: Theek hai, pehle aapka BP check karte hain. *[Doctor checks BP]* Yeh lo... aapka BP abhi 150/96 hai. Thoda high hai. Pulse 88 hai. Temperature? *[Checks]* 98.4 hai, theek hai. Koi aur bimari toh nahi hai? Diabetes, thyroid, kuch?

PATIENT: Nahi sir, kuch nahi hai. Par haan, kabhi kabhi chakkar aate hain.

DOCTOR: Theek hai, dekhte hain. Aapne koi dawai li hai is dard ke liye?

PATIENT: Sir, ghar par ek dost ne Dolo-650 diya tha, par usse koi fark nahi pada.

DOCTOR: Haan, woh sirf fever ke liye hota hai. Is dard mein kaam nahi karega. Chaliye, aap lete jaiye, main kamar aur ghutno ko check karunga.

PATIENT: Jee, sir.

DOCTOR: *[Examines patient]* Haan, yahan dabaane se dard hota hai? ... Haan, theek hai. Ghutno mein thoda swelling bhi hai, haan. Chaliye aap uth jaiye. Yeh jo aap bata rahe hain, yeh lumbar spondylosis aur osteoarthritis ke symptoms hain. Aur BP bhi thoda high hai.

PATIENT: Sir, koi serious toh nahi hai na? Ghabrahat ho rahi hai.

DOCTOR: Koi baat nahi, dhoom-dhaam se dard door ho jayega. Pehle BP ko control karna hoga, aur saath mein pain relief bhi denge. Main aapko kuch dawai likh raha hoon.

DOCTOR: *[Writes prescription]* Pehle, BP ke liye — **Telma-40** (Telmisartan 40mg) subah khali pet ek goli. Dard ke liye — **Diclofenac 50mg** + **Thiocolchicoside 4mg** ki goli, do baar khane ke baad, sirf 5 din ke liye. Saath mein **Shelcal** ek goli raat ko, calcium aur vitamin D ke liye. Aur acidity nahi honi chahiye, toh **Pan-D** ek goli subah khali pet.

PATIENT: Sir, dawai kitni der tak lena hai?

DOCTOR: Telma-40 toh aap regular lena shuru karo, mahine baad BP check karein. Diclofenac wali sirf 5 din. Pan-D aur Shelcal 15 din tak. Aur haan, dawai ke saath **Ecosprin 75mg** bhi shuru karte hain, raat ko, kyunki BP hai toh heart ke liye safe rahega.

PATIENT: Theek hai, sir. Koi aur suggestion?

DOCTOR: Haan, ghutno par garmi ki sikai karo, subah-shaam 10-10 minute. Aur haan, bhaag-daud nahi karna, aaram karo. Namak kam khao, BP high hai. Ek yoga therapist ko dikha lo, kuch back strengthening exercises ke liye. Theek hai?

PATIENT: Jee, theek hai sir. Bahut dhanyavaad.

DOCTOR: Koi baat nahi. Mahine baad BP aur dard check karne aana. Koi problem ho toh turant aana. Shukriya!

**Prescription Summary:**
1. Telma-40 (Telmisartan 40mg) — 1 tab daily empty stomach (long-term)
2. Ecosprin 75mg — 1 tab at night (long-term)
3. Diclofenac 50mg + Thiocolchicoside 4mg — 1 tab twice a day after meals x 5 days
4. Pan-D (Pantoprazole 40mg + Domperidone 10mg) — 1 tab empty stomach x 15 days
5. Shelcal (Calcium 500mg + Vitamin D3 250IU) — 1 tab at night x 15 days

soap_note.subjective: Patient reports lower back pain for 15 days, located in the midline with occasional radiation to the left side. Also reports bilateral knee pain, worse on the left, aggravated by standing. Patient has history of episodic high BP (last reading 140/90) and occasional dizziness. No known diabetes or thyroid disease. Took Dolo-650 (paracetamol) without relief.
soap_note.objective: BP: 150/96, Pulse: 88, Temperature: 98.4°F. Physical exam: tenderness on palpation of lower lumbar spine; bilateral knee swelling noted. No focal neurological deficits.
soap_note.assessment: 1. Lumbar spondylosis likely causing mechanical back pain. 2. Bilateral knee osteoarthritis, left greater than right. 3. Stage 1 hypertension (BP 150/96). 4. Dizziness possibly related to hypertension.
soap_note.plan: 1. Telma-40 (Telmisartan 40mg) 1 tab daily empty stomach for BP control. 2. Ecosprin 75mg 1 tab at night for cardiovascular prophylaxis. 3. Diclofenac 50mg + Thiocolchicoside 4mg 1 tab twice a day after meals for 5 days for pain. 4. Pan-D (Pantoprazole 40mg + Domperidone 10mg) 1 tab empty stomach for 15 days for gastric protection. 5. Shelcal (Calcium 500mg + Vitamin D3 250IU) 1 tab at night for 15 days. 6. Hot compresses to knees twice daily. 7. Low-salt diet. 8. Rest and avoid strenuous activity. 9. Refer to yoga therapist for back strengthening exercises. 10. Follow-up in 1 month for BP and pain assessment; return sooner if symptoms worsen.
